title: trichophyllum elongatum
side_effects: Trichophyllum elongatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, respiratory problems, and stomach discomfort. In some cases, excessive consumption of the plant may lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Additionally, improper use of Trichophyllum elongatum can also cause skin irritation, including redness, itching, and rashes. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes to avoid any potential risks or complications.
